briefdescription.para: The following constants map to the XPAR parameters created in the xparameters.h file.

detaileddescription.para: They are defined here such that a user can easily change all the needed parameters in one place.

briefdescription.para: The following constant defines the address of the IIC device on the IIC bus.

detaileddescription.para: Since the address is only 7 bits, this constant is the address divided by 2.

briefdescription.para: Maximum delay count used for timeout handling.
The instance of the IIC device.
Write buffer for writing a page.
Read buffer for reading a page.
This function writes and reads the data as a slave.

detaileddescription.para: The IIC master on the bus initiates the transfers.

briefdescription.para: This function writes a buffer of bytes to the IIC bus when the IIC master initiates a read operation.

parameterdescription.para: contains the number of bytes in the buffer to be written.

briefdescription.para: This function reads a buffer of bytes when the IIC Master on the bus writes data to the slave device.

parameterdescription.para: contains the address of the data buffer to be filled.
contains the number of bytes in the buffer to be read.

briefdescription.para: This Status handler is called asynchronously from an interrupt context and indicates the events that have occurred.

parameterdescription.para: is not used, but contains a pointer to the IIC device driver instance which the handler is being called for.
indicates whether it is a request for a write or read.

briefdescription.para: This Send handler is called asynchronously from an interrupt context and indicates that data in the specified buffer has been sent.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the IIC device instance.
is the number of bytes sent.

briefdescription.para: This Receive handler is called asynchronously from an interrupt context and indicates that data in the specified buffer has been received.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the IIC device instance.
is the number of bytes received.

detaileddescription.para: Main function to call the IIC Slave example.

briefdescription.para: This file consists of a Interrupt mode design example which uses the Xilinx IIC device and XIic driver to exercise the slave functionality of the IIC device.

detaileddescription.para: The XIic_SlaveSend() API is used to transmit the data and XIic_SlaveRecv() API is used to receive the data.
The example is tested on ML300/ML310/ML403/ML501 Xilinx boards.

The IIC devices that are present on the Xilinx boards do not support the Master functionality. This example has been tested with an off board external IIC Master device and the IIC device configured as a Slave.
This code assumes that no Operating System is being used.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a mta 03/01/06 Created. 2.00a ktn 11/17/09 Updated to use the HAL APIs and replaced call to XIic_Initialize API with XIic_LookupConfig and XIic_CfgInitialize. Some of the macros have been renamed in the IIC driver and some renamed macros are used in this example. 3.4 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. 3.10 gm 07/09/23 Added SDT support. 3.15 vlt 01/27/26 Fixed codespell issues. 3.15 vlt 02/17/26 Added timeout handling to polled wait loops.
